 {"id":"rich_rust-a96","title":"EPIC: Implementation Refinement & Gap Closure","description":"## Objective
Address identified gaps between current implementation and RICH_SPEC requirements, plus implement missing features discovered during validation.

## Background
During spec validation, several gaps and missing features were identified. This epic tracks all implementation work needed to achieve full spec compliance.

## Scope

### Critical Gaps (Must Fix)
1. LRU caching for color parsing - spec requires caching for performance
2. Text.divide() algorithm - must match Python Rich exactly
3. Ratio distribution algorithm - verify integer rounding behavior
4. Hyperlink support (OSC 8) - may be incomplete

### Potential Gaps (Verify First)
1. Style parsing edge cases
2. Markup parser error recovery
3. Table width collapse algorithm
4. Wide character handling in cell calculations

### Implementation Improvements
1. API ergonomics (builder patterns, From impls)
2. Error messages and handling
3. Thread safety considerations

## Success Criteria
- All RICH_SPEC requirements implemented
- No behavioral differences from Python Rich (for in-scope features)
- Clean, idiomatic Rust code

## Dependencies
- Depends on Spec Validation epic completing to identify all gaps","acceptance_criteria":"All identified gaps closed. Spec compliance verified.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:54.039911917-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:57:32.772038744-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:57:32.772038744-05:00","close_reason":"EPIC COMPLETE: All spec compliance gaps verified/closed. Completed: 1) LRU Cache for Color Parsing (already implemented); 2) Text.divide() algorithm (verified); 3) Markup Parser Error Recovery (verified); 4) Hyperlink OSC 8 Support (fully implemented); 5) Style Parsing Edge Cases (verified); 6) Table Width Collapse (verified); 7) Table Width Expand (verified). Remaining cell_len caching is a performance optimization tracked separately under Performance EPIC.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-a96","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:04.859808161-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-ag4","title":"Create E2E Test: Text Wrapping and Justification","description":"## Objective
E2E tests for text wrapping, justification, and the Text.divide() algorithm.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Basic Word Wrap
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_word_wrap() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let text = Text::plain("The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog")
        .wrap(20);
    
    let lines: Vec<_> = text.iter().collect();
    debug!(line_count = lines.len(), "Wrapped text");
    
    for (i, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {
        let width = line.cell_length();
        debug!(line = i, width = width, content = %line, "Line detail");
        assert!(width <= 20, "Line {} exceeds width: {}", i, width);
    }
    
    info!("Word wrap test PASSED");
}
```

### Scenario 2: Long Word Breaking
- Word longer than width
- Verify: Word is broken, not truncated

### Scenario 3: Styled Text Wrapping
- Text with style spans
- Verify: Styles preserved across line breaks

### Scenario 4: Text.divide() Algorithm (CRITICAL)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_text_divide() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let mut text = Text::plain("hello world!");
    text.stylize(6, 11, Style::new().bold());  // 'world' is bold
    
    debug!(original = %text.plain, spans = ?text.spans, "Original text");
    
    let divided = text.divide(&[5, 10]);
    
    for (i, part) in divided.iter().enumerate() {
        debug!(
            part = i,
            content = %part.plain,
            spans = ?part.spans,
            "Divided part"
        );
    }
    
    // Verify spans are correctly split
    assert_eq!(divided[0].plain, "hello");
    assert!(divided[0].spans.is_empty(), "First part should have no spans");
    
    assert_eq!(divided[1].plain, " worl");
    assert!(!divided[1].spans.is_empty(), "Second part should have bold span");
    
    assert_eq!(divided[2].plain, "d!");
    assert!(!divided[2].spans.is_empty(), "Third part should have bold span");
    
    info!("Text.divide test PASSED");
}
```

### Scenario 5: Justification
- Left, Right, Center, Full justify
- Verify: Correct padding/spacing

### Scenario 6: Wide Characters
- CJK text wrapping
- Emoji handling
- Verify: Cell width respected

### Scenario 7: Overflow Methods
- Fold, Crop, Ellipsis
- Verify: Each method works correctly

## Logging Requirements
- Original text and spans
- Wrap width and resulting lines
- Divide offsets and resulting parts
- Span transformations during divide
- Cell width calculations

## Files to Create
- tests/e2e_text.rs
- tests/golden/text_*.txt","acceptance_criteria":"All text scenarios pass. divide() algorithm verified with span preservation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:03.615570733-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T14:33:47.004590631-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T14:33:47.004590631-05:00","close_reason":"E2E Text Wrapping tests complete with 41 passing tests covering all scenarios","labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ag4","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:12.972287839-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ag4","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:54.099529002-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-agw","title":"Add Comprehensive Rustdoc Comments","description":"## Objective
Add complete rustdoc documentation to all public APIs.

## Documentation Standards

### Module Documentation
Each module (color, style, text, etc.) should have:
- Overview of module purpose
- Key types/functions summary
- Usage example
- Cross-references to related modules

### Struct Documentation
Each public struct should have:
- Purpose description
- Fields documentation
- Example construction
- Common use cases

### Function Documentation
Each public function should have:
- Brief description
- Parameters explanation
- Return value
- Panics section (if applicable)
- Example usage
- Errors section (for Result returns)

### Code Examples
- All examples must compile (use ```rust or ```rust,ignore)
- Show common use cases
- Demonstrate error handling where relevant

## Files to Document
- [ ] src/lib.rs (crate overview)
- [ ] src/color.rs
- [ ] src/style.rs
- [ ] src/segment.rs
- [ ] src/text.rs
- [ ] src/markup/mod.rs
- [ ] src/measure.rs
- [ ] src/console.rs
- [ ] src/box.rs
- [ ] src/cells.rs
- [ ] src/renderables/*.rs

## Verification
- Run `cargo doc --no-deps` successfully
- No missing docs warnings with #![warn(missing_docs)]
- Examples in docs compile","acceptance_criteria":"All public APIs documented. cargo doc builds cleanly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:35.808638033-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:35.808638033-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-agw","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-2oy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:47.917432688-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-b7s","title":"EPIC: End-to-End Testing & Validation Infrastructure","description":"## Objective
Create comprehensive end-to-end test infrastructure with detailed logging to ensure rich_rust works correctly as a complete system.

## Background
Unit tests verify individual components, but E2E tests verify the entire rendering pipeline works correctly from user input to terminal output. This is critical for a terminal rendering library where visual correctness matters.

## Why E2E Tests Are Critical
1. **Integration verification**: Components that pass unit tests may fail when combined
2. **Visual regression detection**: Catch rendering changes that affect appearance
3. **Performance regression detection**: Catch slowdowns in real-world scenarios
4. **Cross-platform validation**: Ensure consistent behavior across environments

## Scope

### Test Infrastructure
- Test harness with detailed logging (using tracing crate)
- Golden file comparison for visual output
- Performance regression detection
- CI integration with artifact preservation

### Test Scenarios
- Complete rendering pipeline tests
- Complex document rendering
- Edge case combinations
- Performance benchmarks as tests

### Logging Requirements
- Structured logging with tracing
- Test-specific log levels
- Timing information for each phase
- Memory usage tracking
- Diff output for failures

## Success Criteria
- All major user scenarios covered by E2E tests
- Detailed logs available for debugging failures
- CI runs tests and preserves artifacts
- Performance baselines established and monitored","acceptance_criteria":"E2E test suite operational. All scenarios pass. Logs are detailed and useful.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:43.32014929-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:27:59.103640191-05:00"}

 {"id":"rich_rust-blu","title":"Validate Rule (Horizontal Line) Rendering","description":"Validate Rule component against RICH_SPEC.md and Python Rich source.

COMPONENT PURPOSE:
Rule renders a horizontal line with optional centered title text.

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Rule fills available width correctly
- [ ] Default character is '─' (box drawing)
- [ ] Custom character support
- [ ] Centered title with padding
- [ ] Left-aligned title option
- [ ] Right-aligned title option
- [ ] Title truncation when width insufficient
- [ ] Style application to rule character
- [ ] Style application to title text
- [ ] Empty title (full line) edge case
- [ ] Width=1 edge case
- [ ] Unicode rule characters (double line, bold, etc.)

TITLE POSITIONING:
- Centered: ──── Title ────
- Left: Title ────────────
- Right: ──────────── Title

FILES TO EXAMINE:
- src/renderables/rule.rs
- Python rich/rule.py for reference","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:02.832196764-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T14:39:48.833603445-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T14:39:48.833603445-05:00","close_reason":"Rule validation complete: 16 tests covering all checklist items including edge cases (zero width, width=1, narrow title, multi-char rules, all alignments)","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-bqc","title":"Add Unit Tests for Color Module","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive unit test coverage for src/color.rs.

## Test Cases Required

### Color Parsing
- [ ] Named colors: 'red', 'blue', 'green', all 16 standard colors
- [ ] Hex colors: '#f00', '#ff0000', '#FF0000' (case insensitive)
- [ ] RGB colors: 'rgb(255,0,0)', 'rgb(255, 0, 0)' (with spaces)
- [ ] 256 colors: 'color(128)', 'color(0)', 'color(255)'
- [ ] Invalid inputs: error handling

### Color Conversion
- [ ] TrueColor to EightBit: verify nearest color selection
- [ ] TrueColor to Standard: verify best match
- [ ] EightBit to Standard: verify downgrade
- [ ] Identity conversions (same system)

### ColorTriplet Operations
- [ ] Constructor: ColorTriplet::new(r, g, b)
- [ ] From hex: ColorTriplet::from_hex('#ff0000')
- [ ] To hex: triplet.to_hex()
- [ ] Equality comparison

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Boundary values: rgb(0,0,0), rgb(255,255,255)
- [ ] Out of range: rgb(256,0,0) should error
- [ ] Empty string input
- [ ] Whitespace handling

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/color.rs (add #[cfg(test)] mod tests section)

## Dependencies
- None (self-contained module)","acceptance_criteria":"100% test coverage for color.rs. All edge cases covered.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

All tests must use the tracing infrastructure from tests/common/mod.rs:
1. Call init_test_logging() at test start
2. Use info!() for test phase markers
3. Use debug!() for input/output values
4. Use trace!() for internal state
5. Use error!() only for actual failures

Example:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_parse_named_color() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    info!("Testing named color parsing");
    
    let result = Color::parse("red");
    debug!(input = "red", result = ?result, "Parse result");
    
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    info!("Named color parsing PASSED");
}
```

Run with: RUST_LOG=debug cargo test test_parse -- --nocapture","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:44.565115923-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:06:13.028923001-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:06:13.028928111-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-bqc","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:24.624762818-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
